summary: Spolsky shares a quick travel hack for determining whether a flight will actually be on time. He noticed Continental Airlines' gate display showed an on-time departure, but there was no plane at the gate. By using FlightAware to track the inbound aircraft, he accurately predicted the flight would be 39 minutes late, which proved exactly correct.
key_points: Airline gate displays can be unreliable or overly optimistic about departure times
Tracking the inbound aircraft via FlightAware gives a more accurate picture of actual departure time
The technique proved precisely accurate, predicting a 39-minute delay that matched reality
money_quotes: Being suspicious, I notice that there's no plane at the gate.
Arriving 3:21. A bit later than the display at the gate is showing, and a total of 39 minutes late. And indeed, we took off almost exactly 39 minutes late.
key_insight: You can outsmart misleading airline departure displays by tracking the inbound aircraft on FlightAware to get a realistic arrival and departure estimate.
